message.content:
Review these 4 files for a markdown editor. Check for: parser correctness edge cases, XSS prevention, toolbar cursor position handling, resize drag edge cases, memory leaks in event listeners, accessibility issues, and any other bugs.

Files to review:
1. /Users/yimtaejong/omc-verification/disable-omc/run2/markdown-parser.js
2. /Users/yimtaejong/omc-verification/disable-omc/run2/editor.js
3. /Users/yimtaejong/omc-verification/disable-omc/run2/style.css
4. /Users/yimtaejong/omc-verification/disable-omc/run2/index.html

Return a structured list of bugs/issues with severity (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) and exact file+line references. Focus on actionable fixes, not style preferences.

content.text: Now I have all the information needed to produce the full review. --- ## Code Review Summary **Files Reviewed:** 4 **Total Issues:** 14 ### By Severity - CRITICAL: 2 (must fix) - HIGH: 4 (should fix) - MEDIUM: 5 (consider fixing) - LOW: 3 (optional) --- ### Issues --- **[CRITICAL] XSS via `javascript:` protocol in link href** File: `/Users/yimtaejong/omc-verification/disable-omc/run2/markdown-parser.js:59-63` Issue: `escapeHtml(href)` sanitizes HTML entities but does NOT block `javascript:` URIs. A markdown input like `[click me](javascript:alert(1))` produces ``, which executes JS on click. The same applies to image `src` at line 52 (`[img](javascript:...)` is less exploitable but still a concern). Fix: After extracting `href`, validate the protocol. Reject or strip any URI whose scheme is not `http`, `https`, `mailto`, or a similar safe list. For example: ```javascript function isSafeUrl(url) { const trimmed = url.trim().toLowerCase(); if (/^javascript:/i.test(trimmed) || /^vbscript:/i.test(trimmed) || /^data:text\/html/i.test(trimmed)) return false; return true; } ``` Then guard at line 59: `if (!isSafeUrl(href)) href = '';` --- **[CRITICAL] XSS via un-escaped link label text** File: `/Users/yimtaejong/omc-verification/disable-omc/run2/markdown-parser.js:62` Issue: The link `label` variable is inserted into HTML without escaping: `` `${label}` ``. At this point in `parseInline`, the label text has already been through the step-3 entity escaping (line 44), so *plain* `
